data.title: The Fed is more likely to raise interest rates than to cut – WisdomTree’s Jeremy Siegel
data.description:
The Federal Reserve is more likely heading toward a rate hike than a cut, marking a significant shift in monetary policy expectations, according to Jeremy Siegel, Wharton School professor of finance and WisdomTree chief economist.

In an interview with CNBC, Siegel said he doesn’t see a favorable short-term outlook for rate easing. He pointed to several economic indicators driving his analysis, including an expanding money supply and rising commodity prices.

“I’m looking at the money supply, and I see it expanding, and I’m looking at commodity prices, and I see that expanding fiscal policy, defense spending,” he explained.

These factors, combined with oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) remaining in the upper $90s, create conditions that make it difficult for the Fed to ease monetary policy.

The inflationary pressures extend throughout the economy, with Siegel citing Delta Air Lines (DAL) as an example of how rising fuel costs ripple through corporate America. He noted that Delta (DAL) is budgeting $2B more in fuel expenses, costs that will likely be passed on to consumers through higher fares.

“Facing much more inflationary pressures than he did before,” Siegel said of incoming Fed leadership, describing a “much more difficult position than it looked like three months ago.”

Regarding the leadership transition at the Federal Reserve, Siegel predicted that Jerome Powell will take no action at what he believes will be Powell’s final meeting as chair.

“I think the Fed will do nothing at his last meeting,” Siegel said, suggesting Powell will pass the decision to his successor rather than make a significant policy move on his way out.

For investors, Siegel anticipates a “sideways market” lasting two to three months until geopolitical and economic uncertainties resolve.

However, he cautioned that bonds face greater risk than equities in this environment.

“I’m more worried about the bond rate going up than I’m worried about the stock market going down,” he said.

Despite his near-term caution, Siegel remains optimistic about the long-term outlook and advised investors to hold onto equities rather than sell.

He emphasized that “there are many times in history where the market moves sideways in preparation for a big rise once things get better,” maintaining his bullishness on stocks and his belief that AI will continue to drive economic growth.

More on the U.S. economy

  • Initial jobless claims rise more than expected in past week
  • U.S. Q4 GDP growth estimate further revised down to +0.5%
  • Core PCE inflation comes in slightly hotter than expected in February
  • What’s next for the SP500 after the March jobs beat?
  • New inflation numbers on tap for Thursday and Friday

data.title: Marvell rises after upgrade at Barclays on growth in optical
data.description:
Shares of Marvell Technology (MRVL) jumped about 5% premarket on Thursday after Barclays upgraded the stock to Overweight from Equal Weight citing growth in optical products.

The firm also raised the price target on the stock to $150 from $105.

Analysts led by Tom O'Malley said that Marvell is first and foremost an optical company, and with ports growing rapidly, the market growth carries the name alone. Industry checks suggest optical ports should double in 2026 and double again in 2027, the analysts added.

"Our math implies that Optical could grow ~90% for MRVL this year and next, even with some share shift to AVGO. We lay out a scenario below that shows that even when eliminating MSFT entirely, no unit growth at AMZN, and discounting XPU attach we still get to ~$5 of earnings. We don't agree with those assumptions and in fact are more confident in MSFT ramping after the NVDA NVLink Fusion announcement, said O'Malley and his team.

The analysts noted that Marvell's story will come down to executing on a well-understood and bullish forecast and think that the narrative is shifting more towards optics.

data.title: Sealed Air completes $6.3B deal to be taken private, shares delisted
data.description:
Sealed Air (SEE) on Thursday completed its acquisition by an affiliate of Clayton Dubilier & Rice, ending its status as a publicly traded company and halting trading of its shares on the New York Stock Exchange.

The transaction closed on April 9, with Sealed Air (SEE) becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of the acquiring entity following a merger involving a CD&R-controlled vehicle.

Under the terms of the deal, each outstanding share of Sealed Air (SEE) common stock was converted into the right to receive $42.15 in cash, without interest, excluding certain shares held by the buyer or shareholders exercising appraisal rights.

As a result of the merger, Sealed Air’s stock, which traded under the ticker “SEE,” was suspended prior to market open on the closing date. The company has requested that the exchange formally remove the shares from listing and that regulators terminate its registration, effectively ending public trading.

The total cash consideration paid to equity holders was approximately $6.3 billion, funded through a combination of debt and equity financing.

The deal also triggered a broad restructuring of the company’s capital structure. Sealed Air repaid outstanding borrowings under its credit facilities and redeemed multiple series of senior notes, while also settling obligations tied to receivables financing agreements.

Following the transaction, Sealed Air’s board of directors resigned, with the changes described as routine and related to the completion of the merger rather than any disagreements. The company’s existing management team remains in place to lead the business under private ownership.

Equity-based compensation held by employees and executives, including restricted and performance stock units, was converted into cash-based awards tied to the merger price, subject to vesting conditions in some cases.

With the closing of the deal, Sealed Air will no longer be subject to public reporting requirements once deregistration is completed, marking a shift to private ownership after years as a listed packaging company.

data.title: Eos Energy guides for Q1 revenues of $56M-$57M on record battery output
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) up 8.9% pre-market Thursday after saying it expects to report preliminary Q1 revenues of $56M-$57M, as record shipments and manufacturing output showed continued progress in the company's operational scaling.

Eos (EOSE) also reported quarterly records for shipments, up 17% Q/Q; battery output, up 10.4% Q/Q; and bipolar output, up 10.6% Q/Q.

The company also said bi-polar automation yields improved 22% Q/Q, indicating increased process stability and manufacturing consistency.

Eos (EOSE) said it successfully completed factory acceptance testing for its second battery production line, with initial production targeted for the end of Q2.

Line 2 was purpose-built to expand manufacturing capacity while increasing efficiency, the company said, with enhancements including a single-piece flow configuration, increased process redundancy, and advanced pick-and-place gantry systems to enable faster cycle times and repeatability.

data.title: Top mid-cap Materials stocks ranked by quant ratings ahead of Q1 earnings
data.description:
As earnings season nears, investors are turning to updated quant ratings to gauge how companies stack up on valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and estimate revisions after recent results.

State Street Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLB), meanwhile, has climbed about 14% year to date, leading sector gains even as broader markets remain volatile.
 

Top-quant rated stocks:

  • Aura Minerals (AUGO), Quant Rating: 4.98, Strong Buy.
  • Century Aluminum Company (CENX), Quant Rating: 4.97, Strong Buy.
  • Constellium SE (CSTM), Quant Rating: 4.94, Strong Buy.
  • Centerra Gold Inc (CGAU), Quant Rating: 4.93, Strong Buy.
  • Orla Mining (ORLA), Quant Rating: 4.92, Strong Buy.

Lowest quant-rated stocks:

  • Graphic Packaging Holding (GPK), Quant Rating: 1.41, Strong Sell.
  • Eagle Materials (EXP), Quant Rating: 1.66, Sell.
  • Louisiana-Pacific (LPX), Quant Rating: 1.80, Sell.
  • FMC Corp (FMC), Quant Rating: 1.81, Sell.
  • Hawkins (HWKN), Quant Rating: 1.81, Sell.

data.title: Nio Strategic Metals appoints CEO and CFO
data.description:
• Nio Strategic Metals (NIO:CA) (NIOCF) appointed of Bruno Dumais and Jean-Sébastien Blanchette to its executive suite. 
  • Mr. Dumais is appointed as President and COO while Mr. Blanchette is joining as CFO.
  • The board said it has awarded a total of 4M stock options to directors and officers under its incentive plan, with an exercise price set at $0.27 per share.

data.title: Oracle releases array of agentic applications for finance, supply chain, HR and customer relations
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) released an array of new agentic applications for enterprises covering finance, supply chain management, human resources, and customer relations on Thursday.

The new software suite is called Fusion Agentic Applications, and they are built into Oracle's Fusion Cloud Applications. 

"The new agentic applications are powered by coordinated teams of specialized AI agents that are outcome-driven, proactive, reasoning-based, and engineered for enterprise execution," the company said.

"Finance and supply chain teams are under constant pressure to close faster, respond to disruptions sooner, and deliver more with the same resources, but this is extremely difficult when so much time is still tied up in manual follow-ups, handoffs, and moving work across systems," said Steve Miranda, executive vice president of Applications Development at Oracle. "With agentic applications that can reason, decide, and act against defined objectives, finance and supply chain teams can move from passive productivity to systems that proactively carry work forward, improve working capital, reduce costs and delays, and operate with greater confidence."

The cloud and software provider also introduced new database security tiers for "ultra-critical workloads." The new tiers, dubbed Platinum and Diamond, were designed to handle new security threats arising from AI and quantum computing.

"For the most demanding applications, Oracle Distributed AI Database and Oracle GoldenGate can deliver Diamond-tier availability with disaster failover typically under three seconds," said Juan Loaiza, executive vice president of Oracle AI Database Technologies. "We have also introduced new security capabilities to help address emerging risks from quantum computing and AI-driven data breaches."

Finally, the company upgraded its financial crime and compliance portfolio by securing rights to technology from Lucinity, a software-as-a-service firm that focuses on using AI to investigate and prevent financial crimes.

"Oracle's reach and depth in financial services make it the right platform to bring human-AI-centric investigation capabilities to the institutions that need them most," said Gudmundur Kristjansson, founder and executive chairman of Lucinity. "The platform was built to transform how investigators work, not by replacing them, but by giving them agent-driven execution that surfaces the right context, at the right time."

data.title: Energy Vault acquires 850 MW energy storage portfolio to enter Japan market
data.description:
• Energy Vault Holdings (NRGV) said on Thursday it had entered the Japanese market through a binding deal to acquire a BESS project pipeline from a domestic developer.
  • The deal includes a local energy team and an 850 MW portfolio, providing a multi-year growth pipeline.
  • The portfolio comprises 350 MW of advanced-stage projects and 500 MW of early-stage projects.
  • Advanced-stage projects are slated for construction from H2 2027 and operations from H2 2028.
  • The acquisition positions the company to benefit from Japan’s growing energy storage demand driven by grid constraints and renewable expansion.
  • It plans to tap diversified revenue streams across wholesale, capacity, and balancing markets.
  • A flexible technology platform, including next-generation battery solutions, will support deployment.
  • The global portfolio exceeds 1 GW of energy storage and AI compute infrastructure.
  • More than $180M in annual recurring EBITDA is expected once assets are fully operational over the next 12–36 months.
  • Shares +5.98%.

data.title: DAE, Blackstone Credit form $1.6B annual aircraft investment program
data.description:
Dubai Aerospace Enterprise and Blackstone Credit & Insurance (BX) agreed to launch a new global aircraft investment program, aiming to deploy about $1.6 billion annually into planes leased to commercial airlines, the companies said Thursday.

The initiative, branded “Equator,” is designed to build a diversified portfolio of aircraft leased to airlines worldwide. Under the arrangement, DAE will source aircraft from third parties, while its Aircraft Investor Services unit will manage the assets on behalf of the program.

“Blackstone’s scaled and flexible capital provides a strong foundation to grow our third-party fleet management franchise,” Firoz Tarapore, chief executive officer of DAE, said in the announcement.

The program will be backed by capital from Blackstone Credit & Insurance, including funds managed by ITE Management, which is described as a strategic partner.

Blackstone Credit & Insurance said it expects to provide a range of financing structures for the program, with the goal of supporting investments across different market conditions.

DAE, which had a fleet of about 700 aircraft as of the end of 2025, including more than 100 aircraft under management valued at over $4 billion, operates as both a lessor and a service provider for institutional investors. The company currently serves as a manager across multiple servicing agreements.

Blackstone Credit & Insurance’s infrastructure and asset-based credit platform manages more than $100 billion in assets and invests across sectors including infrastructure, commercial finance, consumer lending and real estate.
